top_findings.summary: The skill requests disabling the sandbox (`dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true`) and broad permissions including `--allow-sys` (system information access), which are excessive for its stated purpose of delegating development tasks through command matching and prompt execution. While permissions like `--allow-read`, `--allow-write`, and `--allow-net` may be justified for file operations and network calls (e.g., to GitHub), full system access and sandbox removal pose significant security risks without clear necessity for the core functionality.
